description: Basic biological life and bodily integrity requirements. Paraphrased from UN Convention Against Torture (1984), WHO Constitution (1946), and general bioethics literature (Beauchamp & Childress, Principles of Biomedical Ethics).

entries.statement: No person may be subjected to physical or psychological torture, regardless of circumstances.
entries.source: UN Convention Against Torture (1984) Art. 1; UDHR Art. 5

entries.statement: Every person has the right to sufficient food and clean water to sustain biological life.
Sleep deprivation used as coercion or punishment constitutes a form of cruel and degrading treatment.
entries.source: European Court of Human Rights; UN Special Rapporteur on Torture, A/HRC/13/39/Add.5

entries.statement: Every person has the right to physical and psychological integrity and to be free from bodily harm.
entries.source: UDHR 1948 Art. 3; Oviedo Convention on Biomedicine (1997) Art. 1

entries.statement: No person may be denied emergency medical care necessary to preserve life.
No person may be subjected to medical or scientific experiments without their free and informed consent.
Every person has the right to be protected from physical violence, domestic abuse, and threats to their safety.
entries.source: UDHR Art. 3; Declaration on the Elimination of Violence Against Women, UN (1993)

entries.statement: Every person has the right to adequate shelter to protect their physical health.
